title: The box that means nothing
testCase: Are the three listings wrapped in a single <div>?
blocks.0.text: The listings have a name now. Next you want all three inside ONE shared box — not each on its own, but the three together, as a section of the page.
blocks.1.text: Try to say it. Around what should the box go? Around the three paragraphs. Except "the three paragraphs" is not a thing. They are three separate things that merely stand next to each other. There is no box holding them together.
blocks.2.text: A class gives a name. It does not turn three things into one.
blocks.4.text: There is a tag for this. It means nothing, looks like nothing, and does nothing. Its only job is to wrap. It is called <div>.
blocks.6.text: Run it. Nothing changes. Absolutely nothing — and that is correct. <div> is invisible by nature. But now the three listings ARE one thing, and that thing has a name. There is something to grab.
blocks.8.text: Above the box is drawn in so you can see what you made. In your editor you will not see it — it comes with CSS. Today you only build the box.
blocks.10.text:
is a block — it takes a whole line, like

. It has a twin that is INLINE: . The same emptiness, only it lives inside a sentence.
blocks.12.text: Again, nothing shows. But now you can grab just the price in every listing — make it green, bold, whatever you decide. Without <span> the price is only letters in the middle of a sentence, with no name and no handle.
blocks.14.text: This is where the temptation starts. You want something bold → you reach for <span class="bold">. It works. Except you already have <strong>, which means "this matters" — while <span class="bold"> means nothing at all. Use <span> only when NO suitable tag exists. A price is not an important word; it is a price. That is why <span> is right here, and <strong> is not.
blocks.15.text: <div> and <span> are the last resort, not the first.
blocks.16.text: Why this matters so much — in the next lesson. I will show you what happens when someone forgets that last sentence.
blocks.17.text: Your turn. Gather the three listings into one named <div>. And if you like, name the prices inside as well.
